channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Linux & Cloud Detection Engineering - TeamPCP Container Attack Scenario
item.description: This publication provides a real-world walkthrough of TeamPCP's multi-stage container compromise, demonstrating how Elastic's D4C surfaces runtime signals across each stage of the attack chain.

item.title: Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management
item.description: The company will invest in R&D, product expansion across AI frameworks, and in scaling go-to-market and sales efforts. The post Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management appeared first on Securi...

item.title: You have to invite them in
item.description: While a garlic and wooden stakes keep the vampires at bay in movies, they won’t save your network once an attacker has been "invited in." Discover why identity is the new frontier of cyber horror in this week’s edition.

item.title: 1stProtect Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding
item.description: The company’s endpoint security platform monitors behavior and verifies user intent to stop cyberattacks in real time. The post 1stProtect Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys
item.description: Latest ScreenConnect version adds encrypted storage and management to prevent unauthorized access to machine keys. The post Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in Cisco FMC and Cisco SCC Firewall Management to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in Cisco FMC and Cisco SCC Firewall Management to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Sec...

item.title: Privacy Platform Cloaked Raises $375M to Expand Enterprise Reach
item.description: Cloaked plans to introduce AI agents designed to act on behalf of users to monitor, manage, and enforce privacy preferences and security postures. The post Privacy Platform Cloaked Raises $375M to Expand Enterprise Re...

item.title: FBI seizes Handala data leak site after Stryker cyberattack
item.description: The FBI has seized two websites used by the Handala hacktivist group after the threat actors conducted a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker that wiped approximately 80,000 devices. [...]

item.title: Ransomware Affiliate Exposes Details of 'The Gentlemen' Operation
item.description: Hastalamuerte leaks The Gentlemen RaaS ops: FortiGate exploits, BYOVD evasion, Qilin split tactics

item.title: CISA urges US orgs to secure Microsoft Intune systems after Stryker breach
item.description: CISA warned U.S. organizations to follow Microsoft guidance to strengthen the Intune endpoint management tool after a cyberattack exploited it to wipe medical technology giant Stryker's systems. [...]

item.title: How Ceros Gives Security Teams Visibility and Control in Claude Code
item.description: Security teams have spent years building identity and access controls for human users and service accounts. But a new category of actor has quietly entered most enterprise environments, and it operates entirely outsid...

item.title: Raven Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding
item.description: Raven’s platform observes applications at runtime to detect anomalous behavior and prevent cyberattacks. The post Raven Emerges From Stealth With $20 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: FCA Updates Cyber Incident and Third-Party Reporting Rules
item.description: The UK’s financial regulator has issued new rules to make incident and third-party reporting clearer

item.title: New ‘Perseus’ Android malware checks user notes for secrets
item.description: A new Android malware called Perseus is checking user-curated notes to steal sensitive information, like passwords, recovery phrases, or financial data. [...]

item.title: Critical Microsoft SharePoint flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: A critical Microsoft SharePoint vulnerability patched in January is now being exploited in attacks, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned. [...]

item.title: Everyday tools, extraordinary crimes: the ransomware exfiltration playbook
item.description: Attackers use trusted tools for data theft, making traditional detection unreliable. The Exfiltration Framework enables defenders to spot exfiltration by focusing on behavioral signals across endpoints, networks, and...

item.title: Building an Adversarial Consensus Engine | Multi-Agent LLMs for Automated Malware Analysis
item.description: Single-tool LLM analysis produces reports that look authoritative but aren't. A serial consensus pipeline catches artifacts and hallucinations at source.

item.title: Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware
item.description: Unit 42 research explores how AI is currently used in malware, from superficial integrations to advanced decision-making, and its future impact. The post Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware appeared first...

item.title: AWS Warns Hackers Have Abused Cisco Firewall Zero-Day Since January
item.description: Notorious ransomware group Interlock has been exploiting a Cisco zero-day bug since January, AWS says

item.title: Hacking a Robot Vacuum
item.description: Someone tries to remote control his own DJI Romo vacuum, and ends up controlling 7,000 of them from all around the world. The IoT is horribly insecure, but we already knew that .

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Uses 6 Flaws, 3 Zero-Days for Full Device Takeover
item.description: A new exploit kit for Apple iOS devices designed to steal sensitive data from is being wielded by multiple threat actors since at least November 2025, according to reports from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG),...

item.title: CISA Warns of Zimbra, SharePoint Flaw Exploits; Cisco Zero-Day Hit in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has urged government agencies to apply patches for two security flaws impacting Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) and Microsoft Office SharePoint...

item.title: Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agents
item.description: Unit 42 outlines the risks of AI ecosystems and allowing AI agents excessive privileges. Learn how to keep your security strategy up to date with these latest trends. The post Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agent...

item.title: Aura confirms data breach exposing 900,000 marketing contacts
item.description: Identity protection company Aura has confirmed that an unauthorized party gained access to nearly 900,000 customer records containing names and email addresses. [...]

item.title: C2 Implant 'SnappyClient' Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: In addition to enabling remote access, the malware supports a wide range of capabilities, including data theft and spying.

item.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
item.description: A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.

item.title: U.S. CISA adds Microsoft SharePoint and Zimbra flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds SharePoint and Zimbra flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added ([1,...

item.title: Amazon threat intelligence teams identify Interlock ransomware campaign targeting enterprise firewalls
item.description: Amazon threat intelligence has identified an active Interlock ransomware campaign exploiting CVE-2026-20131, a critical vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software that could allow an unaut...

item.title: New Ubuntu Flaw Enables Local Attackers to Gain Root Access
item.description: CVE-2026-3888 Ubuntu snap flaw lets local users escalate to root via timing-based exploit

item.title: Marquis: Ransomware gang stole data of 672K people in cyberattack
item.description: Marquis, a Texas-based financial services provider, revealed this week that a ransomware gang stole the data of over 670,000 individuals in an August 2025 cyberattack that also disrupted operations at 74 banks across...

item.title: Researchers warn of unpatched, critical Telnetd flaw affecting all versions
item.description: CVE-2026-32746 is a critical flaw in GNU InetUtils telnetd that allows remote attackers to execute code with elevated privileges Cybersecurity company Dream disclosed a critical flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-32746 (CVSS s...

item.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
item.description: A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks.

item.title: Crypto Scam "ShieldGuard" Dismantled After Malware Discovery
item.description: ShieldGuard Chrome extension posed as a crypto security tool but stole wallets and drained user data

item.title: The Refund Fraud Economy: Exploiting Major Retailers and Payment Platforms
item.description: Refund fraud is now a business, with methods and tutorials sold to exploit return policies for profit. Flare shows how fraudsters turn refunds and chargebacks into a repeatable profit model. [...]

item.title: New DarkSword iOS exploit used in infostealer attack on iPhones
item.description: A new exploit kit for iOS devices and delivery framework dubbed "Darksword" has been used to steal a wide range of personal information, including data from cryptocurrency wallet app. [...]

item.title: The Proliferation of DarkSword: iOS Exploit Chain Adopted by Multiple Threat Actors
item.description: Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified a new iOS full-chain exploit that leveraged multiple zero-day vulnerabilities to fully compromise devices. Based on toolmarks in recovered payloads,...

item.title: Introducing the Agentic Cloud Security Platform
item.description: Orca’s AI agents: Ecosystem engineers for cloud-native apps Ecosystem engineers are living things—animals, plants, insects, etc—that physically modify their environment in ways that create, maintain, or destroy habita...

item.title: Transparent COM instrumentation for malware analysis
item.description: In this article, Cisco Talos presents DispatchLogger, a new open-source tool that delivers high visibility into late-bound IDispatch COM object interactions via transparent proxy interception.

item.title: AI Issues Will Drive Half of Incident Response Efforts by 2028, Says Gartner
item.description: Gartner has urged security teams to get involved in AI projects from the start to avoid costly incident response

item.title: Robotic surgery firm Intuitive reports data breach after targeted phishing attack
item.description: Intuitive suffered a phishing attack leading to a data breach exposing customer, employee, and corporate information. Intuitive is an American company that designs, manufactures, and sells robotic systems for minimall...

item.title: Tracking the Iran War: A Month of Escalation and Regional Impact
item.description: Iran war likely prolonged, increasing cyber threats, energy disruption, and instability, with companies in the Middle East facing higher risk. Follow me on Twitter: @securityaffairs and Facebook and Mastodon Pierluigi...

item.title: Ubuntu CVE-2026-3888 Bug Lets Attackers Gain Root via systemd Cleanup Timing Exploit
item.description: A high-severity security flaw affecting default installations of Ubuntu Desktop versions 24.04 and later could be exploited to escalate privileges to the root level. Tracked as CVE-2026-3888 (CVSS score: 7.8), the iss...

item.title: Apple Fixes WebKit Vulnerability Enabling Same-Origin Policy Bypass on iOS and macOS
item.description: Apple on Tuesday released its first round of Background Security Improvements to address a security flaw in WebKit that affects iOS, iPadOS, and macOS. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20643 (CVSS score: N/A), h...

item.title: CrowdStrike Innovates to Modernize National Security and Protect Critical Systems
AWS completes the second GDV community audit with participant insurers in Germany
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Amazon Web Services (AWS) has completed its second GDV (German Insurance Association) community audit with 36 members from the Germany insurance industry participating, corresponding to...

item.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
item.description: In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.

item.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
item.description: In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.

item.title: 'CursorJack’ Attack Path Exposes Code Execution Risk in AI Development Environment
item.description: CursorJack shows how malicious MCP deeplinks in Cursor IDE can trigger user-approved code execution

item.title: LeakNet Ransomware Uses ClickFix via Hacked Sites, Deploys Deno In-Memory Loader
item.description: The ransomware operation known as LeakNet has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic delivered through compromised websites as an initial access method. The use of ClickFix, where users are tricked into manual...

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited Wing FTP Vulnerability Leaking Server Paths
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a medium-severity security flaw impacting Wing FTP to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active explo...

item.title: Runtime malware detection for AWS Fargate
item.description: Application platform modernization is pushing cloud security teams to increasingly support infrastructure teams in new ways as they adopt new cloud architectures. One avenue that is gaining steam is the use of serverl...

item.title: Detecting CVE-2026-3288 & CVE-2026-24512: Ingress-nginx configuration injection vulnerabilities for Kubernetes
item.description: On March 9, 2026, the Kubernetes ingress-nginx project merged a fix for CVE-2026-3288 (CVSS 8.8 HIGH), a configuration injection vulnerability in the NGINX Ingress Controller. The vulnerability allows any user with pe...

item.title: Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment
item.description: Iranian threat group Boggy Serpens' cyberespionage evolves with AI-enhanced malware and refined social engineering. Unit 42 details their persistent targeting. The post Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment appeared first o...

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
item.description: Dozens of updated, malicious GlassWorm extensions have infested Open VSX, threatening software development supply chains.

item.title: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
item.description: Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Games from evolving threats.

item.title: GlassWorm Attack Uses Stolen GitHub Tokens to Force-Push Malware Into Python Repos
item.description: The GlassWorm malware campaign is being used to fuel an ongoing attack that leverages the stolen GitHub tokens to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories. "The attack targets Python projects — including Dj...

item.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization
item.description: The evolution of Iranian cyber operations in broad context: from custom wiper malware to misuse of legitimate admin tools and more. The post Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization ap...

item.title: Standing up for the open Internet: why we appealed Italy’s "Piracy Shield" fine
item.description: Cloudflare is appealing a €14 million fine from Italian regulators over "Piracy Shield," a system that forces providers to block content without oversight. We are challenging this framework to protect the Internet fro...

item.title: Researchers Warn of Global Surge in Fake Shipment Tracking Scams
item.description: Some of these campaigns are linked to Darcula, a Chinese-language phishing-as-a-service platform

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Days, Router Botnets, AWS Breach, Rogue AI Agents & More
item.description: Some weeks in security feel normal. Then you read a few tabs and get that immediate “ah, great, we’re doing this now” feeling. This week has that energy. Fresh messes, old problems getting sharper, and research that s...

item.title: Attackers Abuse LiveChat to Phish Credit Card, Personal Data
item.description: A social engineering campaign impersonating PayPal and Amazon uses customer support interactions to acquire sensitive info.

item.title: CrackArmor Flaws Expose Linux Systems to Privilege Escalation
item.description: CrackArmor AppArmor flaws let local Linux users gain root, break containers and enable DoS attacks

item.title: Possible New Result in Quantum Factorization
item.description: I’m skeptical about—and not qualified to review—this new result in factorization with a quantum computer, but if it’s true it’s a theoretical improvement in the speed of factoring large numbers with a quantum computer.

item.title: 4 Ways Businesses Use CrowdStrike Charlotte AI to Transform Security Operations
Pickle in the Pipeline: Critical RCE Vulnerabilities in SGLang’s LLM Serving Framework
item.description: The Orca Security Research Pod continuously investigates the security posture of widely adopted AI/ML infrastructure. During a focused audit of LLM serving frameworks, I discovered multiple unsafe deserialization vuln...

item.title: A Guy Who Wrote the Code Died in 2005. I Still Have to Secure It
item.description: The real front line of American cybersecurity is a bidding war on eBay for 30-year-old industrial controllers.

item.title: INC Ransomware Group Holds Healthcare Hostage in Oceania
item.description: Government agencies, emergency clinics, and others in Australia, New Zealand, and Tonga have had serious run-ins with the prolific ransomware outfit.

item.title: DirectX, OpenFOAM, Libbiosig vulnerabilities
item.description: Cisco Talos’ Vulnerability Discovery & Research team recently disclosed vulnerabilities in the BioSig Project Libbiosig library and OpenCFD OpenFOAM, as well as an unpatched vulnerability in Microsoft DirectX. The vul...

item.title: France: National Cybersecurity Agency Reports Ransomware Attack Drop in 2025
item.description: French small and medium businesses remained the organizations most targeted by ransomware in 2025

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling prey to phishing and...

item.title: Infosecurity Europe Announces 2026 Keynote Line Up
item.description: Infosecurity Europe 2026 reveals its keynote line-up, featuring Jason Fox, Shlomo Kramer, Cynthia Kaiser and more, with sessions on AI, cloud security and post quantum threats

item.title: Researchers Uncover ‘LeakyLooker’ Vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio
item.description: LeakyLooker flaws in Google Looker Studio let attackers run cross-tenant SQL attacks on cloud data

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabili...

item.title: Compromised WordPress Sites Deliver ClickFix Attacks in Global Infostealer Campaign
item.description: Over 250 legitimate websites, including news outlets and a US Senate candidate’s official webpage, been compromised to infect visitors with infostealers, warn Rapid7 researchers

item.title: BlackSanta EDR-Killer Targets HR Teams in CV-Themed Campaign
item.description: BlackSanta malware targets HR staff with fake resumes, kills EDR and steals system data

item.title: Researchers Discover Major Security Gaps in LLM Guardrails
item.description: Palo Alto Networks’ Unit 42 has developed a successful attack to bypass safety guardrails in popular generative AI tools

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the U.K., Canada, Korea,...

item.title: Slashing agent token costs by 98% with RFC 9457-compliant error responses
item.description: Cloudflare now returns RFC 9457-compliant structured Markdown and JSON error payloads to AI agents, replacing heavyweight HTML pages with machine-readable instructions. This reduces token usage by over 98%, turning br...

item.title: AI Security for Apps is now generally available
item.description: Cloudflare AI Security for Apps is now generally available, providing a security layer to discover and protect AI-powered applications, regardless of the model or hosting provider. We are also making AI discovery free...

item.title: Dozens of Vendors Patch Security Flaws Across Enterprise Software and Network Devices
item.description: SAP has released security updates to address two critical security flaws that could be exploited to achieve arbitrary code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities in question listed below - CVE-2019-17571 (...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-68613 n8n Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources Vulnerabi...

item.title: What Boards Must Demand in the Age of AI-Automated Exploitation
item.description: “You knew, and you could have acted. Why didn’t you?” This is the question you do not want to be asked. And increasingly, it’s the question leaders are forced to answer after an incident. For years, many executive tea...

item.title: Canada Needs Nationalized, Public AI
item.description: Canada has a choice to make about its artificial intelligence future. The Carney administration is investing $2-billion over five years in its Sovereign AI Compute Strategy . Will any value generated by “sovereign AI”...

item.title: Agentic AI security: Why you need to know about autonomous agents now
item.description: There are many benefits and security risks of deploying agentic AI within organizations. This blog emphasizes the importance of robust risk management and threat modeling to defend against both internal operational er...

item.title: Spinning complex ideas into clear docs with Kri Dontje
item.description: The episode features Kri Dontje discussing her role in translating complex technical cybersecurity topics into clear, accessible documentation, emphasizing the importance of consistency, accuracy, and collaboration wi...

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Two Publicly Disclosed Zero-Days
item.description: March Patch Tuesday sees Microsoft release updates for 79 flaws

item.title: Microsoft Patches 84 Flaws in March Patch Tuesday, Including Two Public Zero-Days
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for a set of 84 new security vulnerabilities affecting various software components, including two that have been listed as publicly known. Of these, eight are rated Critical, and...

item.title: UNC6426 Exploits nx npm Supply-Chain Attack to Gain AWS Admin Access in 72 Hours
item.description: A threat actor known as UNC6426 leveraged keys stolen following the supply chain compromise of the nx npm package last year to completely breach a victim's cloud environment within a span of 72 hours. The attack start...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Highlights Cloud Resilience Gaps
item.description: Data centers — used by both governments and militaries for operations — are now fair game, not just for cyberattacks, but for kinetic attacks as well.

item.title: Microsoft Patches 83 CVEs in March Update
item.description: For a change, there's little in this month's Patch Tuesday that should cause panic, according to security experts.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero...

item.title: Understanding and Reducing AI Risk in Modern Applications
item.description: Identify real AI risk by connecting signals in context across the layers of AI applications.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday for March 2026 — Snort rules and prominent vulnerabilities
item.description: Microsoft has released its monthly security update for March 2026 which includes 79 vulnerabilities, including three that Microsoft marked as “critical.”

item.title: 'Overly Permissive' Salesforce Cloud Configs in the Crosshairs
item.description: Some customers have mishandled guest user configurations otherwise intended to allow third-party access to important — and sensitive — client data.

item.title: AWS European Sovereign Cloud achieves first compliance milestone: SOC 2 and C5 reports plus seven ISO certifications
item.description: In January 2026, we announced the general availability of the AWS European Sovereign Cloud, a new, independent cloud for Europe entirely located within the European Union (EU), and physically and logically separate fr...

item.title: Russian Threat Actor Sednit Resurfaces With Sophisticated Toolkit
item.description: After several years of using simple implants, the Russia-affiliated actor is back with two new sophisticated malware tools.

item.title: Security is a team sport: AWS at RSAC 2026 Conference
item.description: The RSAC 2026 Conference brings together thousands of professionals, practitioners, vendors, and associations to discuss issues covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity—a place where innovation meets collaboratio...

item.title: OpenAI's Promptfoo Deal Plugs Agentic AI Testing Gap
item.description: OpenAI’s latest acquisition addresses a security need Jamieson O’Reilly, security advisor at OpenClaw, raised during an exclusive interview with Infosecurity

item.title: Only 24% Of organizations Test Identity Recovery Every Six Months
item.description: Only 24% of organizations test identity disaster recovery plans every 6 months, Quest Software said

item.title: Cloud Attackers Now Prefer Vulnerability Exploits Over Credentials, Google Cloud Finds
item.description: Google Cloud report details a sharp rise in attackers exploiting software vulnerabilities, including React2Shell

item.title: Ericsson Breach Exposes Data of 15k Employees and Customers
item.description: Ericsson data breach affects 15k employees/customers after third-party service provider compromise

item.title: AWS Security Hub is expanding to unify security operations across multicloud environments
item.description: After talking with many customers, one thing is clear: the security challenge has not gotten easier. Enterprises today operate across a complex mix of environments, including on-premises infrastructure, private data c...

item.title: 'BlackSanta' EDR Killer Targets HR Workflows
item.description: A campaign by Russian-speaking cyberattackers hijacks workflows to deliver security-busting malware, allowing attackers to steal data without detection.

item.title: Investigating multi-vector attacks in Log Explorer
item.description: Log Explorer customers can now identify and investigate multi-vector attacks. Log Explorer supports 14 additional Cloudflare datasets, enabling users to have a 360-degree view of their network.

item.title: Building a security overview dashboard for actionable insights
item.description: Cloudflare's new Security Overview dashboard transforms overwhelming security data into prioritized, actionable insights, empowering defenders with contextual intelligence on vulnerabilities.

item.title: New Malware Approaches, Same Key Indicators
item.description: Key Takeaways Introduction The current conversation around AI and malware is dominated by noise. Security leaders are often told that the threat of AI has fundamentally changed compared to just a year or two ago as la...

item.title: Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Controls
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals AI judges are vulnerable to stealthy prompt injection. Benign formatting symbols can bypass security controls. The post Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Control...

item.title: ShinyHunters Targets Hundreds of Websites in New Salesforce Campaign
item.description: Prolific ShinyHunters group claims to have stolen data from nearly 400 websites in Experience Cloud attacks

item.title: Translating risk insights into actionable protection: leveling up security posture with Cloudflare and Mastercard
item.description: Cloudflare will be integrating Mastercard’s RiskRecon attack surface intelligence capabilities to help you eliminate Internet-facing blind spots while continuously monitoring and closing security gaps.

item.title: March 2026 Patch Tuesday: Eight Critical Vulnerabilities and Two Publicly Disclosed Among 82 CVEs Patched
Uncovering agent logging gaps in Copilot Studio
item.description: During research, we sometimes encounter scenarios that remind us that it's a good idea to trust but verify. In September 2025, we noticed that certain Microsoft Copilot Studio agent settings did not log certain admini...

item.title: White House Cyber Strategy Prioritizes Offense
item.description: In a seven-page strategy document, the Trump administration signaled a shift to preemption and deterrence to handling cyber threats.

item.title: 'InstallFix' Attacks Spread Fake Claude Code Sites
item.description: A fresh cyberattack campaign blends malvertising with a ClickFix-style technique that highlights risky behavior with AI coding assistants and command-line interfaces.

item.title: Are We Ready for Auto Remediation With Agentic AI?
item.description: With the rapid innovations in AI, we are entering an exciting era of automated risk remediation. Learn about security team readiness to leverage agentic AI for threat and exposure management.

item.title: Threat Actor Exploits Flaws and Uses Elastic Cloud SIEM to Manage Stolen Data
item.description: Huntress researchers uncover campaign exploiting vulnerabilities to steal data using Elastic Cloud as a data hub

item.title: Trump Administration Unveils New Cyber Strategy for America
item.description: US national cyber strategy focuses on stronger defenses, countering threats, fostering innovation

item.title: Active defense: introducing a stateful vulnerability scanner for APIs
item.description: Cloudflare’s new Web and API Vulnerability Scanner helps teams proactively find logic flaws. By using AI to build API call graphs, we identify vulnerabilities that standard defensive tools miss.

item.title: UK Launches New Crackdown Unit to Tackle Cyber-Fraud at the Source
item.description: New UK Online Crime Centre will combine expertise from a range of sources to takedown online channels cyber-scammers rely on

item.title: AI Security Startups Dominate New Cyber Innovation Awards
item.description: Over one in five winners of IT-Harvest’s 2026 Cyber 150 are AI security companies

item.title: Command Your Cloud at Mach Speed: Find Orca Security at RSAC 2026
item.description: RSAC is one of our favorite weeks of the year. A chance to get off Zoom, get into the same room, and have the kinds of conversations that actually move the needle on cloud security. This year, we’re heading to San Fra...
